codeReview.summary: The fix does surface `installed_at` to the detail page and addresses the original VAL-DET-001 gap for fresh databases, but it also changes the persisted `skill_installations` schema without adding any migration path for already-initialized app databases.

issues.description: This feature adds a required `created_at` column to `skill_installations`, and both `get_skill_installations()` (`SELECT *` into `SkillInstallation`) and `upsert_skill_installation()` now assume that column exists. But `init_database()` still only uses `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, which does not alter an existing table. Any user who already had a pre-fix database will keep the old 5-column schema, so the next rescan/install/detail query will fail at runtime with errors like `table skill_installations has no column named created_at` instead of showing the repaired detail page.

summary: Fail. The timestamp UI fix resolves the original metadata omission on fresh databases, but it breaks already-initialized databases because the new `skill_installations.created_at` column is never migrated into existing schemas.
